I have top pair with Q♠8♠ on Q♣-4♥-5♦. Both opponents checked to me after Elon 3‑bet preflop and Pro cold‑called. This is a good spot to bet for value and protection — my kicker is weak so I want to charge worse queens, small pairs and any backdoor draws while using position. Elon’s 3‑bet range is skewed strong (AQ+, big pairs, some sets) while Pro’s cold‑call range is wider (small pairs, suited connectors, broadways) and will pay off a reasonable c‑bet. Pot is 2700; I’ll size ~50% pot to extract value without overcommitting: bet 1350. Plan: if I get a large raise (polarized to sets/two‑pair/AQ) I’ll fold; if called I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn and continue for thin value on blanks or check/control if a scary card arrives.

Facing a flop bet of 1350 into a pot of 4050 with ace-high no pair no draw on 4h5dQc. My A♠T♦ completely misses, holding minimal showdown value against Minni's wide button calling range post my 3-bet (Qx, pairs 66+, suited connectors like 67/34/45, Ax suited). Board favors her range heavily with top pair combos, straights, and pairs. Pro's check from BB could be wide defenders or traps, but short stack (~5k) risks jamming if I raise. Calling OOP multiway commits me further with poor playability on turning broadways/draws that improve opponents more. Raising has low fold equity (Minni floats/aggressive per history, Pro can shove better), turning hand into bluff with poor equity realization late (hand 136/150, stacks deep except Pro). Pot control via check failed as expected; folding preserves deep stack for premium spots in final hands, exploiting opponents' aggression elsewhere.

I hold Pocket Aces (AhAd) on a Q-5-4 rainbow flop. After trapping pre-flop by cold-calling a 3-bet, I am well-disguised. Minni betting 1,350 (half-pot) into two opponents indicates significant strength, likely Top Pair (AQ/KQ). My remaining stack is 4,925, and the pot is 4,050. If I just call, the pot grows to 5,400 with me having only 3,575 behind (SPR < 0.7), creating an awkward stack depth where I might miss value if she checks back the turn for pot control. By raising all-in now, I maximize value against her calling range (Qx) which is likely to call given the pot odds, and I protect against any bad runouts. My hand is too strong to slowplay further given the wetness of action.

I c‑bet the flop with top pair (Q♠8♠) to 1,350 and Pro check‑raised to 4,925. The pot is 8,975 and I must call 3,575, which gives me ~28.5% break‑even equity. Pro’s check‑raise here is heavily weighted toward value (sets: 4♣4♠/5x, QQ, and strong Qx like KQ/AQ, maybe occasional two‑pair) — a range that dominates my weak‑kicker top pair. My realistic equity versus that polarized/value‑heavy range is well under the ~28–29% needed (roughly in the ~20–25% area), and the board is fairly dry so I don’t have strong draw equity to justify calling. A shove/raise would be spewy and often get called by better hands. Given the size of the raise, the range I put Pro on, and stack preservation for better spots, folding is the correct, +EV choice here.

3-handed on button with deep effective stack (250BB), facing what appears to be a small bet/open of 100 into 150 pot from SB Pro (89BB). My K5o is a strong candidate for a 3-bet: live king blocker reduces villain's premium KK/ AK, decent high-card strength and playability postflop. Pot odds make calling marginal without initiative, but raising ~3x achieves fold equity against Pro's wide SB opening range (likely >50%), isolates if called by BB Minni (history shows passive calling stations). Takes down small pot immediately often; if contested, strong position to realize equity. Late tournament (14 hands left), stealing small pots builds stack without high variance. Min raise 200 too small; sizing to 300 balances value/bluff.

I am in the Small Blind with JdTh facing a 3bb raise from the Button (Elon). JTo is a marginal hand that is easily dominated by the opener's value range (AJ, KJ, QJ, AT, KT) and plays poorly out of position. While the pot odds are reasonable, the realizable equity is low due to being out of position against a large stack, and the threat of a squeeze from the Big Blind is present. At this stage of the tournament (Hand 137/150) with the shortest stack (though still deep at ~89bb), minimizing variance in marginal spots and preserving chips for better positional situations is prudent. The standard play here is to fold.

Elon opened to 300 from the button and Pro folded — it’s heads-up. I’m in the big blind with Jc 9s; I’ve already posted 100 so calling costs 200 more (total commitment 300) and would make the pot 650. That’s ~30.8% pot odds (200/650). J9o has reasonable equity vs a standard button opening range and with ~260BB effective stacks the implied odds are good. A tiny min-raise to 500 would be weak and I don’t want to isolate or commit with a marginal hand; I’ll call to see a flop, play cautiously out of position, fold to large preflop 3-bets, and continue only with pair/draws or favorable board textures.

Flop A♣ 3♠ T♣, I'm OOP with J♣9♠ — no pair, only jack-high and only a runner‑runner club/straight (very low equity). Elon opened wide from the button so he'll c‑bet often, but this texture heavily favors Ax/Tx/KQ type continuations. Betting as a bluff OOP into his likely continuing range is a poor use of chips; I want to keep the pot small and realize equity. I will check to control the pot and see a c‑bet. Plan: call small c‑bets (roughly ≤1/3 pot, ~200–225) to see a turn that can improve me to a real hand (J or a club), fold to larger bets (>~1/2 pot, ~>325) that are likely value-heavy, and only lead or bluff on later streets with a pair or a credible draw.

Heads-up on flop after my button raise and Minni's BB call. Board Ac 3s Tc is somewhat coordinated with club flush draw and possible gutshots/straights (KQJ). My Ks5c is king-high with no pair or draw, but as preflop raiser in position, my range includes strong aces (AK, AQ, AT), tens, and broadways that hit this board. Minni's check suggests weakness from a wide defending range (suited connectors, Ax, small pairs, offsuit broadways). History shows Minni passive, calling stations but folding to postflop aggression often. C-bet small (~60% pot) for value/thin value vs weak Ax/Tx/draws and fold equity vs pure air/missed hands. King blocker reduces villain's AK/AQ. Deep stacks (250BB eff), position allows control if called; checking risks giving free cards to draws/improvers and loses value when ahead ~40-50% time. Late tournament (13 hands left), building stack via small pots low variance. Size 400 balances bluff/value, denies equity.

Board A♣ 3♠ T♣; I'm OOP with J♣9♠. Elon c‑bets 400 into a 1050 pot — calling 400 would require roughly 27–28% equity (400/(1050+400) ≈ 27.6%). My realistic equity to improve to a reasonably strong hand by showdown is marginal: ~6 immediate pair outs (three jacks + three nines) gives ~24% to hit by the river, plus a small (~4%) runner‑runner club flush chance — total ~28% at best. Even if my raw equity is near the breakeven point, most of my improvements (a single jack or nine) are second‑pair type holdings behind Ax/Tx and will be difficult to realize OOP against an aggressive button who barrels. I explicitly planned to call small c‑bets (~≤1/3 pot) and fold to larger continuation bets (>~325); this 400 bet exceeds that threshold. With stacks deep but no compelling implied‑odds and poor board texture for my hand, preserving chips and waiting for a better spot is correct. I fold.

I am on the Button with [2d 5h], which is one of the weakest starting hands in poker (7-2 offsuit is famously the worst, but 5-2 offsuit is statistically very similar in poor equity). With 3 active players and being the short stack (though still deep with ~89 BBs), there is no incentive to try to steal the blinds with zero equity backup. The risk of being called or 3-bet by the deep-stacked blinds (Minni and Elon) is too high compared to the small reward of winning the blinds. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Button folded — I'm heads-up with the small blind. J♠4♣ is marginal, but heads-up I should be opening a wide range and take the initiative because I will have position postflop. I raise to 300 (≈3× the BB) to apply fold equity, isolate Elon, and keep the pot size manageable with a speculative hand. Plan: fold to a large, committed 3‑bet (J4o has poor equity vs a strong 3‑bet); if called use position to c‑bet dry boards and control the pot on coordinated/risky boards. This is an exploitative, low‑risk aggression to steal the blind and play postflop from the button.

Pocket Tens (TsTd) is a premium hand that plays well multiway or heads-up, especially against a small blind raise which is often wide-ranging including suited connectors, Ax, broadway, and bluffs. Pro has folded, so it's heads-up with Minni who has been aggressive but folds to pressure often (e.g., recent hands). With deep stacks (~250BB), 3-betting to 900 (3x her raise) builds the pot, charges her wide range, and takes initiative. If she calls, we have position and good equity; if she 4-bets, we can reassess. Calling is viable for set-mining/implied odds, but 3-betting maximizes value and fold equity late in the tournament (hand 138/150). Folding is never correct.

Elon’s re-raise to 900 is a committed 3‑bet after my 300 open. I would have to call 600 into a 1,200 pot (break‑even ≈33%). With J♠4♣ off‑suit I’m heavily dominated by most of Elon’s value 3‑bet hands (A/K/Qx, better Jx) and my raw equity vs a realistic 3‑bet range is below the required break‑even. 4‑betting is not sensible — I’d have to put in 1,500+ with a very weak hand — and calling simply sets up difficult post‑flop decisions despite having position. I’ll preserve my deep stack and wait for a better spot. Fold.

3-handed on the button with Qc3c — suited queen-three is marginal but playable in position. With the blinds 50/100 and the pot 150 (I must call 100), opening is preferable to limping: I want to take the initiative, steal the blinds, and play the rest of the hand in position. I’ll raise to 300 (≈3x the BB). That sizing is standard here — big enough to fold out worse hands and get value from weaker holdings, small enough relative to my deep stack to keep options open. Postflop plan: continuation bet dry boards, proceed cautiously on connected/wet boards (check/call or check-fold to big aggression); if faced with a large 3-bet (rough guideline: ~3x my open or >900 total) or an all-in from the short stack, I’ll give up Q3s often since it’s frequently dominated. Overall this is a low-risk, +EV open to exploit position and fold equity.

Hand #139, late in tournament (139/150 hands). In SB with Kh6h suited. Minni raises to 300 from button (3BB), wide range possible. Pot 450, call 250 OOP. K6s has some equity but poor playability OOP post-flop, low kicker limits top pair value, vulnerable to domination. Pro in BB yet to act with shorter stack (88BB), could squeeze. Deep stacks but prefer folding marginal hand OOP to preserve stack for premium spots in final hands. No strong reason to 3bet bluff or call.

I am in the Big Blind with 3s 9h, facing a 3bb raise from the Button (Minni) after the Small Blind folded. My hand is extremely weak (93o) and poorly playable out of position. The pot odds are approximately 30.7% (calling 200 to win 650), but my equity against a standard button opening range is barely meeting that, and realization of equity out of position with trash is very difficult. Given the 3x sizing, this is a standard fold.

K9o is a strong hand on the button in a 3-handed late tournament spot with only 11 hands left. Position is excellent, and the pot offers great odds (calling 100 into 150). Raising builds the pot, applies fold equity against the blinds (Pro shorter stack at 8775, Minni deep), and I can play post-flop profitably in position. Minni limped BB, Pro likely limped SB, wide range. Standard raise size around 3x the to-call for value and isolation.

I am in the Small Blind with 6d 5d, a suited connector. The Button (Elon) has raised to 300. Given that there are no antes in play (Pot is 450 = 300 BTN + 100 BB + 50 SB), the pot odds for calling from the Small Blind are not favorable enough (needing ~36% equity) to play this hand out of position against an aggressive opponent. Flat calling would also invite a squeeze from the Big Blind. While I need to accumulate chips to catch up, playing marginal hands out of position is not the optimal strategy. 3-betting is an option but risky against the chip leader. Folding is the most prudent decision to preserve my stack for better spots.

I have T8 offsuit (8s Td) in the big blind facing a Button open to 300. This is marginal but very playable heads-up — decent straight/two‑pair potential and reasonable equity vs a wide opening range. Calling the additional 200 (total commit 300) is the best line: it defends a wide BB range, keeps the pot controllable out of position, and takes advantage of deep stacks for postflop play. A 3‑bet with T8o would be too loose OOP and would bloat the pot without a clear edge; folding is too tight given the pot odds (~450:200 ≈ 2.25:1, ~30.8% equity required). Plan: see the flop and proceed only with top pair, strong draws, two pair/sets, or good equity; otherwise fold to heavy aggression on dry boards.

Flop: K♠ 6♥ 4♦. I hold T♦8♠ — I completely missed. No pair and only very remote backdoor outs (need both 7 and 9 for a straight; need two more spades for a flush). Elon opened from the button preflop, so his continuing range is weighted toward Kx, AQ/AJ, broadways and pocket pairs — this flop favors the raiser. Out of position with a marginal hand, I don't want to bloat the pot with a bluff that has little fold equity vs a wide calling range. Best line is pot control: check and see what Elon does. If he checks back I get a free turn; if he c-bets small (≈≤1/3 pot) I can consider a call to see a turn with backdoors, but I'll respect larger bets and fold to significant aggression. Preserve my deep stack for better spots.

Top pair with King-9 on a dry Ks-6h-4d flop, checked to me in position heads-up vs Minni's wide BB calling range after my preflop raise. Board is rainbow with no strong draws, but low straight draw possible (5-3). My range is perceived strong as button raiser. Value bet to charge worse Kx (KQo-KJo, KT), pairs (77-JJ, 55), draws (65s, 53s, 75s). Protect against Ax chasing, overcards. 2/3 pot size builds pot late tournament while denying equity. Minni deep but can fold weak hands. Plan to barrel turn if improves or check-fold weak turns.

I completely missed the K♠‑6♥‑4♦ flop with T♦8♠. I defended preflop to see the flop and checked for pot control — now Elon c‑bets 450 into the ~650 pot (~69% pot). My hand has no pair and only very remote backdoor straights (would need two perfect cards); there is no realistic flush draw. Calling 450 to win ~1,550 would require ~29% equity, and versus a button opener’s c‑bet range (Kx, broadways, mid pairs, lots of value) my equity is well below that. Raising OOP with air has almost no fold equity and would bloat the pot unnecessarily. With a healthy stack and better spots to pick later in the match, folding and preserving chips is the correct play.

Current tournament state: 3 players left, I am the short stack with ~87BB (8775 chips) while opponents have ~25k each. Hand 141/150. I am on the Button with Ks3s. Analysis: In a vacuum, K3s is often an open-raise from the Button in a 3-handed game due to position and suited blocker potential. However, looking at recent table dynamics, the blinds (Minni and Elon) are deep-stacked and have been defending their blinds frequently (calling or 3-betting). Since my fold equity appears lower than standard given their stack sizes and recent activity, and K3s plays poorly when called (dominated by better Kings, weak kicker), the EV of opening here is marginal to negative compared to preserving chips for a better spot in the final 9 hands. Maximizing stack size at this stage involves avoiding bleeding chips in marginal spots against aggressive big stacks.

I have 6♦4♠ in the small blind, button folded — heads-up vs the big blind. Pot is 150 and it only costs me 50 to see a flop (cheap odds). With deep stacks and being out of position, I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal, easily-dominated hand by min‑raising; calling keeps the pot small and lets me realize equity. I'll complete the call (total committed 100). Postflop I'll continue only with meaningful improvement (pair/two‑pair/strong draws) and fold to heavy aggression when missed.

Hand 141/150, deep stacks (258 BB effective). In BB facing SB limp after BTN fold, heads-up. Td6c is speculative offsuit with straight potential but low equity vs typical SB limp range. Checking realizes equity on flop cheaply with position post-flop, avoids bloating pot OOP with marginal hand. Raising risks playing OOP with dominated range or folding to 3-bet. Pot 200 already good for implied odds if we hit.

Flop 4♣ 9♥ 8♥ and I hold 6♦4♠ — only bottom pair with a poor kicker. I limped preflop to see a cheap flop, so my range is wide but my actual hand is vulnerable to 9x/8x, overpairs and hearts/straight draws. Betting here would bloat the pot and invite raises from better hands for little value; a small probe-bet has thin fold equity and makes future decisions harder with a marginal holding. I’ll check to control the pot and see how the big blind reacts — call small sizing to protect vs draws, fold to significant pressure, and only get aggressive if I improve (two-pair/trips) or pick up clear fold equity later.

Hand 141/150, very late, deep effective stacks (~250BB). Heads-up vs SB (Minni) who limped pre and checked flop. My Td6c has a gutshot straight draw (7x for 6-7-8-9-Td) with ~4 outs (~8% equity), plus backdoor club flush draw. Board 4c-9h-8h is highly coordinated (strong heart flush draw, gutshots like 5-7 or 10-J). SB limp-check range polarized: weak pairs/A-high floats or monsters trapping. With position and initiative post-check, small semi-bluff bet denies equity to weaker draws/floats, extracts value from worse, and pot committed lightly if called. Betting 100 (half-pot) balances range, folds out better ace-high, builds pot for implied if we hit. Check risks giving free card to superior draws or check-raise trap. Prior preflop check was for realization; now exploit position.

I flopped bottom pair (4♦) with a poor kicker on 4♣ 9♥ 8♥. Elon’s bet is very small (100 into the ~300 pot) so calling 100 gives me ~25% pot odds — cheap to continue against a wide big‑blind range that will include plenty of bluffs and heart/straight draws. Checking/calling keeps the pot controlled and lets me realize equity and re-evaluate on the turn: I’ll fold to significant turn aggression if I miss, call small turn bets, and only get aggressive if I improve to two‑pair/trips or pick up clear fold equity. A raise here bloats the pot with a marginal hand and invites stronger hands to raise back, so the correct line is a call.

I have 6♦4♠ — bottom pair with a poor kicker on 4♣ 9♥ 8♥ 8♣. Elon bet very small on the flop and I called, but the turn pairs the board (another 8) and he checked to me. My hand has limited showdown value vs a flop-betting range (any 9, any 8 = trips, overpairs, bigger pocket pairs) and betting here would either fold out only the weakest hands or get me check‑raised/put to an uncomfortable decision against better holdings. With deep stacks and a 400‑chip pot, I want pot control and to realize equity (I improve to trips or two‑pair only on a 4 or 6). I’ll check and re-evaluate the river — call small/medium bets that look like bluffs but fold to large, committed aggression. For now: check to keep the pot small and avoid bloating with a marginal hand.

Hand 141/150, final stretch, deep stacks (~250BB effective). Heads-up vs SB Minni (passive limp-check-call flop, check turn). Board 4c-9h-8h-8c pairs 8s, dynamic: flush draws miss unless clubs/hearts, straights limited. Td6c: live gutshot to 7 (~4 outs, 8% equity), backdoor projects nothing strong. Her range: weak A-high, KQo+, small pp, missed draws (J10, 75s) after limp-call small C-bet. My flop c-bet (semi-bluff/value thin) got called; turn check gives initiative. Half-pot bet 200 denies equity to her floats/better aces/pairs (e.g., 77, 55, A9), extracts from worse (87, 97), balances as value (98s, 88, sets). High fold equity vs passive line; position protects if called. Checking risks free river card to catch up or pot control but misses fold equity/value. Late tourney: apply pressure to build stack without showdown risk.

Board is 4♣ 9♥ 8♥ 8♣, I hold 6♦4♠ — bottom pair with a poor kicker, out of position. Elon raised the flop and now bets a small 200 on the turn into a 600 pot. I have only 5 clean river outs to meaningfully improve (three sixes + two fours) = 5/46 ≈ 10.9%. Calling 200 to win 800 requires ~25% equity (200/(600+200)), so pure pot-odds math shows this is -EV unless Elon is bluffing a very high percentage of the time. His flop raise + small turn bet is consistent with many value hands (9x, 8x, overpairs) and some thin value or semi-bluffs, so I should not gamble more chips with a vulnerable bottom pair and bad kicker. Raising would bloat the pot and get me in an unlucky spot. Preserve stack and fold.

Three-handed on the button with Jh8c. In heads-up/short-handed play I open my range significantly — J8o is playable in position and I want to seize initiative and pressure the blinds (Pro is a shorter stack, Elon defends wide). Table standard open has been ~300 for me, so I raise to 300 (3x BB): that’s small and constructive, gives me position post‑flop and a chance to steal or isolate. Plan: fold to a large 3‑bet (we’re not calling big 3‑bets out of position with J8o); if Pro shoves (~8.6k) I’ll fold this holding; if called I’ll continuation-bet good flops and play cautiously multiway. This raise balances aggression and risk while maximizing EV in late-stage hands.

3-handed late in tournament, button raise to 3BB from Minni who is aggressive in late position based on history. A6o is a standard defend from SB vs BTN open: Ace provides blocker to strong Ax hands, decent equity vs wide BTN range (which includes many weaker Ax, suited connectors, broadway). Pot odds excellent (250 to call into 450 pot, ~3:1). OOP but hand has playability postflop (top pair potential, gutshots/straights). BB Pro has 86BB and folds frequently preflop per history, unlikely to squeeze wide. Calling to realize equity, control pot postflop or fold if miss. 3-bet not ideal as 6 kicker weak for value and not best bluff candidate with remaining hands. Folding too tight with Ax OOP vs BTN.

I am in the Big Blind holding King-Four offsuit (K4o) facing a Button raise to 300 and a Small Blind call. The pot is currently 700, and I need to call 200 to stay in. This offers me pot odds of 3.5 to 1, requiring roughly 22% equity to break even. While my raw equity against two wide ranges might slightly exceed this threshold (around 23-25%), my realizable equity is significantly lower due to being out of position against the pre-flop aggressor in a multi-way pot. K4o plays poorly post-flop; hitting a King often leaves me with kicker trouble, and missing connects with very few boards. As the short stack in the late stage of the tournament (Hand 142/150), preserving chips is crucial, and avoiding marginal, likely negative expected value (-EV) spots due to poor playability is the correct strategy.

Flop hits my hand perfectly for middle pair with ace kicker (top kicker), blocking strong Ax hands. Board 6h-Qd-7c has straight draw potential (e.g., 8-9, 5-8 open-enders) and some Qx draws, but rainbow otherwise. Vs Minni's wide button opening range (broadway, suited connectors, Ax, pairs), I have strong equity: ahead of Qx with worse kickers, missed Ax/high cards, pure draws/bluffs. OOP SB, betting protects against draws, gets value from worse pairs/draws, and builds pot with showdown value/disguised strength. Check risks giving free cards to draws or check-raise spot which is less ideal OOP vs aggressive Minni who cbets wide per history. 1/2 pot size (400) balances value/protection, denies equity, and keeps pot manageable with deep stacks (258BB effective). Late tournament (hand 142/150), but strong spot to apply pressure vs shorter Pro stack indirectly.

I opened wide on the button with Jh8c and missed this flop (Qd 6h 7c). Elon (called preflop wide and now leads) has bet 400 into a 1100 pot — calling 400 gives me ~26.7% pot‑odds (400 to win ~1500). I have very little immediate equity but I’m in position and can realize equity or use position to float and take the pot on later streets. A raise here would bloat the pot with a weak hand and little fold equity; folding is too nitty given the price and my initiative. I’ll call 400, see the turn, and continue only if I pick up a pair, a usable draw, or a good spot to bluff; otherwise I’ll fold to significant turn aggression.

Turn brings Ah, pairing my Ad for top pair Aces and pairing the 6h with my 6c for two pair aces and sixes - a very strong hand heads-up. Board now 6h-Qd-7c-Ah features possible straight draws (8x9x, 5x8x) that may have hit runner-runner, Qx top pair, weaker Ax, sets (unlikely QQ/77/66 given action), or bluffs/draws from Minni's wide BTN range that called flop cbet. Equity very high vs her continuing range: crushes Qx, worse Ax (AJ-A9, AT), pairs below, missed draws. OOP first to act, betting extracts value from worse made hands/draws while protecting against any villainous straight draws or runner-runner threats; checking risks free river card or giving her chance to rep strength. Sizing 1100 (~3/4 pot) balances value from Qx/Ax/draws, denies equity, keeps pot growable on river with deep effective stacks (~240BB). Late tourney but no ICM pressure with 3 players/9 hands left - maximize EV by getting called by worse.

I opened wide and called the small flop lead, but the turn ace and Elon’s raise to 1100 into a 2600 pot is a clear sign of value (A×, Q×, two‑pair or better) or a very strong line. I have Jh8c with no pair and only about 6 clean outs (three J’s + three 8’s) — roughly 12–14% to improve on the river. Calling 1100 to win ~3700 requires ~30% equity (1100 / (2600+1100) ≈ 29.7%), so this is a negative‑EV call. A raise or shove would bloat the pot with almost no showdown equity and is too risky three‑handed at this stage. I’ll preserve my stack and fold to this significant turn aggression.

AhJh is a premium suited broadway hand on the button in an unopened pot (assuming SB folded or limped minimally, given pot 150 and to-call 100). Deep stacks across the board, with position advantage. Late in tournament (hand 143/150), but still time to accumulate. Standard play is to raise for value and to thin the field, targeting 3x the BB. Raising to 300 isolates weaker hands from blinds and builds the pot with my strong equity realization in position. Folding or limping is suboptimal.

I hold a pair of Threes (3c 3s) in the Small Blind. The Button (Elon) has opened for 300 (3BB). While pocket pairs have set-mining potential, playing a low pair Out of Position (OOP) against a 3x raise is marginally profitable at best and often losing. I need to call 250 chips into a pot that will be around 700-800. My implied odds are technically present (stack is >30BB), but the risk of being squeezed by the Big Blind or simply bleeding chips by check-folding on most flops makes this a difficult spot. Against a 3BB open, GTO ranges often fold 22-44 from the SB. I will elect to fold and preserve my stack for a better opportunity.

Elon (button) opened to 300; pot is 450 and I have 7♠5♣ in the big blind. Calling costs 200 into a 650 pot (≈23% break-even equity). 7‑5 offsuit is a poor hand OOP with low playability and weak blockers — it realizes equity badly versus a button opening range and is vulnerable to c-bets and multi-street pressure. A 3‑bet bluff is technically possible but this hand lacks good blockers or suit to make it an attractive, low-risk bluff; given deep stacks and many hands left I should avoid unnecessary variance. Fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the Button with Kd9h. In a 3-handed game, K9o is well within the standard opening range (top ~45% of hands). I have position on both opponents (Minni and Elon), who are deep-stacked. Opening allows me to potentially steal the blinds or play a pot in position. I will raise to 2.5 BB (250 chips) to exert pressure while managing risk.

6♠4♥ is a very weak, unsuited hand and horribly positioned — I’m out of position to both players if I call. Pro’s open to 250 is a standard-sized raise and will be weighted heavily toward hands that dominate 6-4 offsuit; if Elon (BB) defends we’ll be multiway with very poor equity and bad reverse implied odds. A 3-bet or call isn’t profitable here — better to conserve a healthy stack (24k+) and wait for a stronger spot. Fold.

Facing a small raise to 250 total (150 to call) from the button by Pro (~84BB stack). Qs8c is a marginal but defendable hand in BB vs BTN wide range. Pot odds are excellent: 150 into ~550 (~21.4% equity needed). Equity vs typical BTN 35-45% range is ~35-40%, sufficient. OOP but deep stacks allow postflop playability. Late in tournament (hand 144/150), defending small adds to stack without much risk. Pro likely wide; no need to 3bet weak offsuit Q8. Folding too nitty; calling optimal to realize equity and control pot.

Facing the flop of 8♦2♥T♣ as OOP in BB after defending wide vs BTN open. Middle pair with Q kicker (Qs8c) on a relatively dry but coordinated board (possible J9 str8 draws, gutshots). Pot 550, deep effective stacks (Pro ~83BB). Button range very wide postflop: Ax, KQo+, broadway, suited connectors, pairs. My equity strong vs polarized cbet range (~45-55% depending on sizing). Late tournament (144/150), 3-handed, ICM pressure low with big stacks but Pro shorter. Leading OOP risks getting check-raised by better pairs/draws or check-folded by air, losing deception. Checking realizes equity, induces bluffs/checks from weak hands, allows pot control. Plan: check-call small cbets, check-fold large; check-raise monsters if any. Optimal balance vs aggro BTN.

I opened pre-flop with K9o on the Button and was called by the Big Blind. The flop comes 8d 2h Tc. This texture connects somewhat with the Big Blind's calling range (pairs of Tens or Eights, straight draws like J9 or 97). While I have King-high overcards and some backdoor straight potential, I do not have a strong immediate draw or made hand. C-betting here is an option, but checking back allows me to realize my equity for free, avoid getting check-raised, and potentially induce bluffs or call down on later streets if I improve. Pot control is preferable with a marginal hand like King-high in position.

On the turn 6♣, the board is 8♦ 2♥ T♣ 6♣, adding a club flush draw and completing some straight draws (e.g., 9-7, J-9 gutshot now open-ended). I hold middle pair (8♣) with Queen kicker (Q♠8♣), decent but vulnerable OOP vs Button's wide range after he checked flop (suggesting weakness: missed draws, weak Ax, floaters, or traps). Pot 550, deep stacks (Pro ~83BB). Betting risks getting raised by better hands/draws or check-raised; thin value vs his checking range inefficient as many calls from worse are unlikely. Checking realizes equity against bluffs/missed draws, controls pot, and keeps initiative for river decisions. Late tournament (144/150, 3-handed), no need to force action with marginal strength; preserve stack for better spots. Plan: check-call small river bets from weak value/draws, fold to large aggression.

On the Turn (8d 2h Tc 6c), the pot is 550. I have King-high and a gutshot straight draw (need a 7). My opponent (Elon, BB) has checked twice. Checking back the flop indicated I wasn't strong or was exercising pot control. The turn 6c adds a club draw and straight possibilities (97, 75). Since I have decent equity (4 outs to the straight, plus 6 outs to hit a pair which might be good) and K-high has some showdown value against missed draws, betting risks getting check-raised and denied equity. Checking back guarantees I see the river for free, maximizing my chance to improve or win at showdown cheaply.

River 5♠ is a somewhat blank card, bringing a spade flush draw that doesn't complete but pairs no board cards. Board now 8♦2♥T♣6♣5♠ has possible straights (e.g., T9/98/76/47), sets (TT/66/55? unlikely), but Pro's double check-back suggests capped range: Ax high, missed draws (flush/ str8 like J9/KQ), smaller pairs (77-JJ unlikely to check twice), bluffs. My Q♠8♣ (middle pair Q kicker) beats most of that (~60-70% equity vs checking range). Pot 550 small relative to deep stacks (mine 268BB, Pro 83BB), late (144/150 3-handed). Checking risks free showdown but Pro may have showdown worse; betting thin value/protection extracts from Ax/KQ/weak pairs, denies equity. Small bet ~2/3 pot (375) balances, gets called by worse, folds air. Avoids check-raise risk from nuts unlikely in check range. Maximizes EV by denying free river cards already passed, builds stack.

On the River, the board is 8d 2h Tc 6c 5s. I hold Kd 9h. The opponent bets 375 into a pot of 550. My King-high has shown weakness by checking back the flop and turn. The opponent's line (Check, Check, Bet) often indicates a hand with some showdown value like a small pair (2x, 5x, 6x) or a missed draw trying to steal. However, the Big Blind calling range connects well with this low, connected board (pairs of 8s, 6s, 5s, 2s, Tens, or Ace-high). I only beat pure missed broadway draws (like QJ) or lower busted straight draws (like J7s/74s). The bet size is nearly 70% of the pot, which isn't a trivial amount to call with just King-high. Without a specific read that the opponent is over-bluffing river spots, folding is the disciplined play to preserve my 83BB stack for better opportunities.

I’m on the button in a 3-handed game with Td5d — a marginal but playable suited hand that gains a lot from position and deep stacks. With blinds at 50/100 I should be opening a wide range from the button to take the initiative and steal the blinds. Limping/calling 100 hands off the initiative and lets the small blind (Elon) — who has shown aggression — seize control. I’ll open to a standard ~3x BB (300) to apply pressure, keep the pot manageable, and capitalize on positional postflop play. Plan: if single opponent calls I’ll c-bet favorable T/diamond/low-card flops and pot-control or check when missed; fold to a large 3-bet (strong range) but consider calling a smallish 3-bet because of deep stacks and implied odds. Raising maximizes fold equity and puts me in position to extract value or take the pot down early.

Hand 145 of 150, very late in tournament with only 3 players left. In SB with Ac3h offsuit, a weak ace with poor kicker and no suited potential. Facing button min-raise to 300 (3x BB). Button ranges are wide, but calling OOP multiway (BB likely to defend or squeeze) is poor with this hand - dominated often by better aces, poor playability postflop. Pot odds ~1.8:1 require ~35% equity, but realized equity OOP lower. 3bet bluff possible with ace blocker, but stacks deep, ICM pressure low with big stack (273BB), better spots remain with 6 hands left. Pro BB stack 82BB may 3bet or call wide. Optimal: fold to preserve stack and wait for premium hands.

I am in the Big Blind with Q6o. The Button (Minni) has raised to 300 (3bb). This is a larger sizing than a min-raise, which worsens my pot odds (requiring 200 to call into a pot of 450, offering roughly 2.25:1). Q6o is a weak offsuit hand that is easily dominated by the opener's Qx range and plays poorly out of position. While a call might be borderline acceptable against a min-raise or with a wider closing range, against a 3x open, tightening my defense range is correct to preserve my stack (currently the shortest at ~82bb) and avoid difficult post-flop situations with reverse implied odds. Folding is the disciplined decision.

3-handed on the button with Kd5h pre-flop. Excellent position for a steal raise. K5o has a king blocker, decent high card value, and plays well post-flop in position. Blinds are small relative to stacks (Pro 82BB, Minni 242BB, me 273BB), and with only 5 hands left, accumulating chips aggressively without showdown risk is optimal. High fold equity vs blinds; if called, I have position and live cards. Raising to 3x BB size standard for button opens 3-handed. Minni and Pro have been active but button pressure often works.

I am in the Small Blind with 7h 3c, a very weak hand (73o). The Button (Elon) has raised to 300. Given the poor playability of this hand, my positional disadvantage being out of position post-flop, and the standard aggressive opening from the Button, I have no incentive to defend. Folding is the only standard and profitable play here to preserve chips.

I’m in the big blind with J♣9♥ facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450; I must add 200 (300 total) to call. Stacks are deep (~240–270 BB), so postflop playability matters. J9o is marginal but has decent equity and good playability against a wide button opening range, and the call gives correct pot odds (~30% required) to see a flop. I don’t want to 3-bet a hand that’s often dominated and bloat the pot out of position. Plan: call 300 to see the flop; if I hit (pair, two pair, or strong draws) I’ll play aggressively, otherwise fold to heavy continuation aggression on A/K/Q-heavy boards or proceed cautiously with backdoors.

Check. I have J♣9♥ on a very dry 3♦-2♦-4♣ flop — no pair, no meaningful draw (only a backdoor club). I’m out of position vs the button who opened wide preflop, so I don’t want to bloated the pot with air. Checking keeps the pot under control, lets him c-bet (so I can fold to real strength or float against a small probe), and preserves deep stacks for hands where I have real equity. Plan: if Elon leads small (~≤1/3 pot) I’ll consider a float to try and take the pot later or call if the price is right; if he fires medium/large I’ll fold (I have no showdown value). If he checks back I’ll take a free turn card and only invest further if I pair or pick up a real draw.

Flop of 3d-2d-4c is very coordinated, hitting BB calling ranges hard with straights, OESDs (e.g., 56, 65), flush draws (AdXd), and low pairs. Minni's check from BB after calling my button raise suggests weakness—strong hands might lead or check strong/medium. My Kd5h is king-high with a diamond blocker (reduces villain's Ad/Kd flush draws) and position. No made hand or strong draw, but king-high has showdown value and blocks top of villain's range. With deep stacks (~270BB), few hands left (5 remaining), and Pro short and folded, check back for pot control risks giving free cards to draws that beat me, while betting small exploits Minni's likely wide check range, generates fold equity, denies equity, and builds pot in position if called. Standard 1/3-pot c-bet size (pot 650) folds out worse like Ax-high, gutshots, and weak pairs. History shows Minni folds to aggression post-flop often (e.g., Hand 140, 137). Maximizes EV by taking it down or proceeding favorably.

Elon (button) opened and then fired a small c-bet (225 into ~650, ~1/3 pot) on a very dry 3♦-2♦-4♣ board. I have J♣9♥: no pair and only a backdoor club, so I’m not valuetowning or 3-betting OOP. Because the bet is small and stacks are deep, calling is a cheap float — good pot odds (~20% to call) to see a turn and try to take the pot away from a wide c-betting range. Plan: call the 225 to keep the pot manageable and reevaluate on the turn — if Elon checks I can take the initiative; if he barrels small I can continue as a float; if he fires a large, polarized second-barrel I’ll fold unless I’ve improved (pair/club). Avoid bloating the pot with air OOP, so call now.

Turn pairs my J (J♣9♥) — I now have top pair with a mediocre kicker. Elon checked the turn after a small flop c-bet, which looks like range weakness or pot control from a wide BTN opening range. Betting here gets thin value from worse one-pair hands (A‑x, K‑x, Q‑x, some smaller pairs) and charges any lingering diamond/runner draws while keeping the pot manageable with deep stacks. I’ll make a half‑pot sizing to extract and deny free cards; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate the river, if he check‑raises big he’s likely polarized and I can fold.

Facing a 600 bet into 1700 on the turn with Kd5h (king-high, no pair or draw) on a board of 3d-2d-4c-Js. The flop was highly coordinated with flush and straight draws; Minni's check-call of my small c-bet suggests a medium-strength hand or draw, but her turn lead after checking indicates improvement or a semi-bluff turning into value/thin value. Possible hands: wheel straight (A5, 5x), pairs like 44/33/22 sets/trips, Jx pairs, diamond flush draws, or 56 for straight. My king-high has some live card value but minimal equity (~15-20% vs plausible range) against this line. Pot odds require ~26% equity to call (600 to win 2300), which I don't have. Raising is a pure bluff with no fold equity given her aggression; check-fold optimal for pot control and stack preservation. With 4 hands left, 3-handed, and short-stacked Pro, avoid spew—live to steal blinds or double up with premiums. Folding maximizes EV long-term.

I am on the Button with 5s 4h in a 3-handed game. With 3 hands left in the tournament and being the shortest stack (though still deep with 82BB), my primary goal is to pick better spots. 54o is very weak and below the standard opening range even on the button, especially against two large stacks who have shown willingness to defend and 3-bet. Folding is the correct disciplined play here to preserve chips for better opportunities in the final few hands.

I’m in the small blind with 7♦5♥ — a weak offsuit hand. The pot is 150 and it only costs me 50 more to complete (total commitment 100). Raising to 200 risks bloating the pot out of position and inviting a wide defend or 3-bet from Elon; folding surrenders 50 dead chips unnecessarily. I’ll call cheaply to see a flop and proceed cautiously: fold to heavy aggression on dry boards, continue only with a pair/two-pair or strong draw, and try to control pot size OOP.

Hand #147 pre-flop, in BB with 3d Th (offsuit, weak hand with poor playability and low equity). Pro (button) has folded, Minni (SB) has limped. Pot 200, no call needed. Late in tournament (hand 147/150), 3 players left, stacks deep (mine 26725, Minni 24850, Pro 8225 but folded). Raising trash like T3o is -EV spew; better to check and realize any equity on flop in position vs SB, or fold to aggression. Preserve stack for final hands.

I have middle pair (7♦) with a weak 5 kicker on a Q♠ 7♣ 3♣ flop. I’m heads-up and out of position vs Elon (BB), and the board contains two clubs so there are live flush draws plus overcards (Qx) that beat me. I want to deny free cards to club draws and extract thin value from worse hands (random overcards, Jx, Tx, some small pairs) while keeping the pot controllable OOP. A half‑pot protection/value bet (~100 into 200) accomplishes that — it charges draws, gets value, and doesn’t commit a large portion of my stack. Plan: if he folds I take it; if he calls I proceed cautiously on the turn (check-call vs small bets, fold to large raises), and I’ll respect a check‑raise as strong.

Late in tournament (hand 147/150), deep stacks (~267BB effective). Defending BB vs SB limper wide. Flop 7c Qs 3c gives bottom pair (3s) on paired-club board. No draws, but pair beats bluffs/air/draws in Minni's wide limping range (overpairs unlikely, Qs possible). Min-bet 100 into 200 preflop pot offers ~4:1 direct odds, excellent implied given depth/position (act last postflop). Calling realizes equity cheaply, controls pot size, can fold to turn aggression or bet if checked to. Raising too thin (weak kicker, vulnerable to Qx/club); folding too tight vs small bet from SB. Preserve aggression for later streets/manuvers with few hands left.

I check. On the flop I bet to protect my middle pair and was called; the turn J♣ now brings a third club to the board (7♣ Q♠ 3♣ J♣). I have no club and my 7♣5♠ pair is vulnerable to Qx, Jx, any pocket pair >7 and — importantly — any two‑club hands that flopped a made flush. Leading again risks a check‑raise or large value bet from a made flush and would bloat the pot out of position with a marginal hand. Checking controls the pot, lets me see Elon’s reaction (allowing me to call small protection/value bets or fold to polarized/large bets), and preserves stack flexibility for the river. Plan: check now; call small turn bets (to keep worse pairs and bluffs) but fold to large bets/check‑raises that strongly represent flushes or two‑pair+; if checked to on the river I’ll re‑evaluate and only thin‑value bet safe, non‑club runouts.

Late in tournament (hand 147/150), heads-up vs Minni (SB limper) with deep effective stacks (~250BB). Board 7c Qs 3c Jc is highly coordinated: two-tone clubs (flush draw), QJ broadway, possible straights (T9, KT). Hold bottom pair 3s (3d Th) with no kicker, no draws—vulnerable to any Qx, Jx, clubs, overpairs, sets. Flop min-bet by Minni (weak value/draw?) called cheaply in position. Her turn check suggests weakness (missed draw, air, or trap), but betting thin value here risks inducing bluffs/raises from better hands or draws. Pot 400, checking realizes equity cheaply to river showdown (beats her bluffs/A-high worse), controls pot size, preserves stack for final 3 hands. Betting offers fold equity but low EV vs wide calling range; prefer check to avoid spew on scary board.

I check. I only have a single pair (7s) with a weak 5 kicker on a K‑Q‑J‑7‑3 board with three clubs. Elon called my small flop bet and then checked both turn and river — that line is consistent with a marginal made hand (Qx/Jx/Kx), a slow‑played strong hand, or a missed draw. Very few worse hands will call a river bet (small pairs like 3x or weak showdown hands), while almost every better hand (K/Q/J, two‑pair, any made flush) either already beats me or will call a value bet. I also don’t block clubs, so representing a flush is not very credible. Betting here risks getting called by hands that beat me and folding out the rare worse hands, so the highest EV line is pot control: check to showdown and save chips. If Elon now bets a small thin value I’ll consider a call; I’ll fold to a large polarizing river shove that strongly represents a made two‑pair/flush.

River check by Minni after checking turn continues to represent weakness in her SB limping range (missed draws, air, marginal Qx/Jx without strong kicker). Board extremely draw-heavy (clubs flush, broadway straights possible with QJT/K), but my bottom pair (3s) with T kicker beats bluffs, weaker pairs (77/33 sets unlikely from limp), and Ax-high. Deep effective stacks (~250BB), only 3 hands left—check realizes equity cheaply at showdown without risking stack to better hands/raises. Betting thin value risks spew vs calling range including Qx/Jc/clubs; fold equity insufficient to justify sizing into pot control. Preserve chips for final hands where ICM/position matter more.

On the button with Q♦7♦ I want to be the aggressor — this is a reasonable steal/open-raise spot in 3‑handed play. Both opponents have posted blinds (pot 150) and I have position on the flop, plus Q7s has playable backdoor flush/straight potential. Elon (SB) has shown frequent preflop aggression but also a wide range; Pro (BB) is shorter-stacked and will defend narrower. I’ll open to a standard 3x-ish size (300) — larger than the min-raise to get decent fold equity and to avoid giving good odds to callers. Plan: fold to a large 3-bet from Elon, otherwise play postflop in position and proceed cautiously with a single queen or strong diamond draws.

Hand #148, very late in the tournament (148/150). In SB with Kc2d, a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability OOP. Minni's button raise to 300 is likely wide, but K2o has low equity against her range and dominated by any Kx she might have. Pro in BB has a short stack (8125), which could lead to volatility, but calling 250 OOP with this hand risks too much without position or strong potential. Folding preserves my healthy stack (26575) for better spots in the final hands. Standard fold.

Hand 148/150, 3-handed. I am in the Big Blind with K8o facing a min-raise from the Button (Minni). The pot offers odds of 2.25 to 1 (calling 200 to win 450), requiring approximately 30.7% equity. Against a wide Button opening range in a 3-handed game, K8o has significantly more equity than this (likely around 40-45%). Given my stack depth (81 BBs) and being out of position, a 3-bet is unnecessary and bloating the pot with a marginal hand is risky. Folding is too tight given the price. The most profitable play is to defend the Big Blind by calling.

Hand 148/150, 3-handed. I defended the Big Blind with K8o against a Button raise. The flop comes 7c As 4s. This board texture heavily favors the pre-flop raiser (Minni), who has all the strong Ax combos in their range. As the defender out of position with only King-high and a weak backdoor flush draw, I have no reason to lead out (donk bet). My standard line is to check to the aggressor. If they bet, I will evaluate based on sizing, but currently, my plan is simply to check.

I raised preflop and got called by the BB. Flop A♠ 7♣ 4♠ gives me middle pair (7♦) with a Q kicker and position. This is a good continuation-bet spot to extract value from worse pairs, charge backdoor draws and fold out missed overcards, while keeping the size modest because an Ace in villain’s range beats me. I’ll make a ~1/2-pot c-bet (325) to achieve fold equity and deny free cards. If I face a sizable check-raise I’ll fold (short-stacked BB’s check-raise will usually indicate a strong Ace/set); if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and proceed cautiously.

The current hand is 148 of 150. I am in the Big Blind with K8o on a 7c As 4s flop. I checked, and Minni (Button) bet 325 into a pot of 650. The pot odds are 325 to win 1300 (25%). While I have King-high and a backdoor flush draw (8 of spades), the Ace heavily favors the pre-flop raiser's range. Minni is likely to have an Ace, a flush draw, or a better pocket pair often enough that floating out of position is negative EV. As the short stack with only a few hands left, I need to preserve chips for better opportunities to shove or high-equity spots, rather than calling off with marginal holdings out of position.

On the button with 4c5s suited connectors, facing a small blind limp/complete (weak range) and big blind check. Deep stacks, excellent position. 45s has strong post-flop playability with straight and flush potential, great implied odds multiway. Raising takes initiative, isolates the limper's weak range, can steal the pot immediately (pot 150 for my 200 raise over the 100), or play profitably post-flop if called. Folding is too tight from button; flat-calling risks getting squeezed or playing out of position OOP players check back. Min-raise too small, standard 3x to 300 builds decent pot while keeping fold equity high. Near end of tournament (149/150), but stacks deep enough to play speculatively if needed. Optimal aggression to maximize stack.

I am in the Small Blind with Pocket Threes (3s 3h). The Button (Elon) raised to 300 (3bb). I have 7,875 chips. While implied odds technically exist for set mining (stack-to-call ratio > 30), playing a low pocket pair out of position against a 3x open is difficult. I will likely face overcards on the flop and struggle to realize my equity. Additionally, the Big Blind is yet to act and could squeeze. Given there are only 2 hands left and the goal is to maximize stack size, preserving the 250 chips is better than taking a marginal, likely negative EV spot out of position.

Elon opened to 300 from the button and the SB folded, so it’s heads-up. I hold Js5d — a marginal holding but defendable out of the big blind against a standard 3bb open. Calling the 200 (300 total committed) gives about ~31% pot odds to see a flop and keeps the pot controllable with both stacks deep. I don’t want to 3-bet light with J5o and risk bloating the pot or running into a 4-bet; postflop I can proceed cautiously and fold to heavy aggression if I miss. Call to defend and realize equity postflop.

I hold J♠5♦ on a K♣ 8♣ 7♥ flop — no pair, no club, and no realistic straight draw. Elon was the preflop raiser from the button so his range contains a lot of Kx and club combos that connect well with this board. Pot is 650 and both stacks are deep, so I don’t want to bloat the pot with a naked bluff that has poor blocker value. Best line is to check and control the pot: fold to a standard continuation bet, and only continue if I pick up a clear improvement or face an extremely tiny probe bet that gives correct pot odds to call. If Elon checks back I can re-evaluate on the turn with a free card. Checking preserves chips and avoids getting exploited out of position with a bluff that has low EV.

Flop of 8c-Kc-7h is coordinated with flush draw potential (two clubs) and straight draws (e.g., 5-6, 9-10, 6-9). My 4c5s holds a gutshot straight draw to the 6 (for 4-5-6-7-8) and a backdoor club flush draw with my 4c, giving some semi-bluff equity. As the preflop raiser from the button with position heads-up vs BB defender (wide calling range including pairs, Ax, suited connectors, draws), Minni's check suggests weakness or trapping, but BB check-raises are rare. Pot is 650, deep stacks (26k effective), hand 149/150 so ICM less relevant yet. Checking realizes equity cheaply and controls pot with marginal hand, inducing bluffs on turn/river or improving for free. Betting risks check-raise (board favors draws) and commits more chips to weak showdown value; thin value bet unlikely vs range. Optimal: check back, maintain balance in c-bet range (betting stronger hands like Kx, sets, two-pair), play pot in position post-flop where I can bluff missed draws or value bet improvements.

Check. I have J♠5♦ with no pair, no club and almost no showdown value on K♣ K♠ 8♣ 7♥. Elon was the preflop raiser and checked both flop and turn — that can be either a missed hand or a trap, but either way betting as a bluff here has poor fold equity (his range includes many Kx, paired hands and club draws) and we have no meaningful blockers. The pot is tiny relative to our stacks, so pot control is correct: take a free river to try to pair up or to see what he does. Plan: if he bets the river and I haven’t improved, fold to reasonable pressure; if I pair a J or 5 on the river I can consider a small value bet or call a small bet.

On the turn with board 8c-Kc-7h-Ks, my 4c5s has a gutshot straight draw (4 outs to 6 for 4-5-6-7-8), backdoor club flush draw, and minimal showdown value. Preflop raiser who checked flop, now checked to again heads-up vs BB defender with wide range (pairs below kings, draws, Ax). Minni's double check suggests weakness (no K likely or would bet flop for value/protection; draws might bet turn). Pot 650, deep stacks (~25k effective), hand 149/150 so ICM minimal pressure but conserve stack for final hand. Checking realizes equity cheaply OOP check-raise risk low after double check; induces bluffs on scary paired board/river or lets me improve for free. Betting (even small semi-bluff) risks villain folding better or check-raising (trap with Kx/set); thin value absent. Maintains balanced checking range (weak hands/draws), exploits position by seeing river cheaply to bluff/value if improves or missed draws. Optimal: check, pot control, play final cards in position.

The river (8♣ K♣ 7♥ K♠ 9♦) completes the hand and I have J♠5♦ — no pair, no club, no meaningful blockers. Action checked to me; pot is 650 and both stacks are deep. Elon opened preflop and then checked flop and turn, a line that contains a lot of Kx and other medium/strong hands that will simply check back a paired board. A bluff here is thin: a half‑pot bet (~325) needs >~33% fold equity to break even and I don’t have the blockers or story to credibly fold out many of his holdings. Conversely, I have almost zero showdown value so calling a bet is only correct versus a vanishingly small probe. With one street left in the tournament I don’t want to bloat the pot with a very weak bluff. Optimal line is pot control — check and preserve the stack. If he bets into me I’ll fold to normal sizing (only consider calling an extremely tiny bet that gives correct pot odds).

River 9d is a blank that doesn't complete obvious draws (no flush besides clubs which she might have bet, limited straights like 5-6 or T-J unlikely after triple check). Board paired kings, scary for villain with top pair or draws. My 4c5s is pure air but as preflop raiser who checked back streets (balanced range including bluffs), now great spot to bluff OOP triple-checker with wide BB calling range (low pairs, missed draws, Ax). Her checks suggest weakness/no showdown value or trap but traps rare after checking flop on drawy board. Pot 650, effective stacks ~25k deep, bet 40% pot (260) exploits fold equity high, risks little relative to stack. Hand 149/150, chip lead position maximizes EV aggression preserving upside for final hand. Check risks her better air/Ax taking it down; bluff denies equity, consistent with button strategy. If called, minimal damage OOP weak range crushes.

Board: 8c Kc 7h Ks 9d. I hold J♠5♦ — no pair, no club, no backdoors. Elon opened preflop, checked flop and turn, then made a small 260 river bet into a 650 pot (pot now 910). To call 260 I need ~22% equity (260 / (910+260) ≈ 22%). His line and sizing are strongly consistent with a value-heavy range (Kx/trips, sets, occasional turned pairs) with only a small portion of pure bluffs (missed club draws, etc.). My hand has almost no showdown equity versus that range and very few meaningful blockers, so a call would be -EV. A raise would be a thin bluff with poor fold equity and a high chance of being called by any king or better. I preserve chips for a better spot and fold.

This is the final hand of the tournament (Hand 150/150). I am on the Button with a very weak hand, Ten-Three offsuit. My stack is 7875, which places me in 3rd place behind Minni (25150) and Elon (26825). Even a double-up would not improve my rank significantly to overtake 2nd place. Therefore, there is no ICM pressure to take a massive risk, nor is there a strategic reason to play a trash hand like T3o. The highest EV play is to fold and secure the current stack size.

Heads-up on the very last hand with J♥7♠ in the small blind. The button folded so it’s just me vs Elon (big blind). J7o is marginal, but heads‑up it’s playable and I want to maximize final stack EV rather than be passive. A minimum raise to 200 (total) applies pressure and picks up the dead blind/pot some of the time while not over‑committing if Elon fights back. If he folds I win the 150 pot; if he calls I still have reasonable equity and can proceed postflop. I’ll avoid an all‑in or a limp/call which surrenders initiative and gives him cheap flops. Action: min‑raise to 200.

Hand #150, final hand. In BB with 84o offsuit, a very weak hand with minimal equity and poor playability postflop. Minni raised to 200 from SB, likely a steal attempt given position, but calling 100 into 300 pot offers insufficient odds for this trash hand. No strong blockers or suited/connectors. Folding preserves stack against better ranges. Optimal play to maximize EV.
